name: BBQ Pork Lo Mein Dxdg05a
recipeIngredient: 2 cups Bok choy (cut into 1/2 inch pieces)
recipeInstructions:
1. Cook noodles in plenty of boiling water until al dente. Drain and toss with sesame oil. Set aside.
2. Combine the sauce ingredients. Set aside.
3. Heat a wok, add oil, stir-fry ginger for about 10 seconds until fragrant.
4. Add bok choy, bean sprouts, and pork. Stir-fry for 2 minutes.
5. Add broth, then when hot, add noodles. Cover and cook for 2 minutes.
6. Add sauce, stir well, and serve.
Variations: Instead of pork, use leftover cooked roast beef, lamb, shrimp, or chicken. Other fresh vegetables in season can be used in place of the sprouts and bok choy. From "Dim Sum" by Ruth Laws. 8 oz Lo Mein noodles. Jane Harris (DXDG05A).
